mission: Determine whether the standard neutral puncture Z_k data can produce a nonzero integral index degree by any additive theorem on the current stack.

theorem_1_neutral_puncture_group.statement: For fixed puncture number N and Chern-Simons level k, the standard puncture label space is the neutral subgroup K_N = {a in (Z_k)^N : Σ_i a_i = 0}. This is a finite abelian torsion group, isomorphic to (Z_k)^(N-1).

theorem_2_integer_homomorphism_zero.statement: Every group homomorphism from the finite torsion group K_N to the torsion-free group Z is zero. Therefore any additive puncture-charge-to-integer lift from the standard neutral Z_k data to an integral index degree vanishes identically.

theorem_3_exact_remaining_escape.status: derived / strongest exact current frontier sharpening
theorem_3_exact_remaining_escape.statement: The standard puncture-U(1) sector cannot yield nonzero n_punct by any additive, charge-homomorphic theorem. Any positive route must therefore be genuinely extra: either a non-additive lift, extra continuous geometric data, or a different puncture-resolved operator sector.

imports.previous_lift_map: {a_i in Z_k with Σ_i a_i = 0 mod k} -> n_punct in Z \ {0}

theorem_3_exact_remaining_escape.killed_route: additive lift from neutral Z_k puncture charges to integer degree
still_not_derived: a different full puncture-resolved operator sector beyond standard neutral Z_k labels

final_boundary.status: derived / strongest current Paper 35 additive-lift no-go
final_boundary.safe_statement: The standard puncture-U(1) route is now dead in its natural additive form. Neutral Z_k puncture labels cannot generate a nonzero integral index degree by any additive theorem. Any further positive route would have to be genuinely non-additive or use extra data beyond the standard puncture charge sector.
